spec_match.rationale: The page now exercises the corner-shape shorthand with 1-4 values, the physical per-corner longhands, named keywords including square, and superellipse(<number>) values including negative and zero values accepted by the current spec.

interactive.rationale: Mode buttons, shape buttons, shorthand arity controls, longhand selects, superellipse and radius sliders, component switches, copy handling, parser checks, and computed readouts all update the live preview.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede now matches the actual controls: selecting corner-shape values, switching shorthand/longhand mode, dialing border-radius, and inspecting accepted CSS.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo surfaces zero-radius no-effect behavior, valid negative and zero superellipse() numbers, four-value shorthand mapping, per-corner longhand mapping, and malformed value rejection through exact CSS parser checks.

references.rationale: The see-also section includes the required chromestatus.com/feature/5357329815699456 link and a CSS Borders 4 corner-shape reference.

design_system.rationale: Local component styles use the shared --bg-*, --text-*, --border-*, --accent-*, and shadow tokens, with button background overrides paired with color overrides.

summary: Repaired after chrome-devtools-mcp verification on http://localhost:3000/v147/corner-shaping-corner-shape-superellipse-squircle/shape-picker/: load was console-clean, the old page only exposed one shorthand value at a time, and current Chrome accepted negative and zero superellipse() values. The concept now covers shorthand arity, per-corner longhands, support/failure parser checks, zero-radius fallback behavior, references, and design-system compliance.
